title: MUBS HR Manual 2024
summary: The official Human Resource Manual for Makerere University Business School, amended in 2024. It governs recruitment, appointments, performance management, promotions, leave, discipline, grievance handling, welfare, and terms of service for academic and administrative staff.
manifestoAlignment: The manifesto is grounded in this manual. Pillar 01 (Unity) draws on grievance and representation provisions. Pillar 02 (Welfare) on terms, benefits, and working conditions. Pillar 03 (Growth) on promotions, performance appraisal, and study leave. Pillar 04 (Sustainability) on transparent processes and documented institutional practice.

title: Universities and Other Tertiary Institutions Act, Cap 210
summary: Uganda's principal law governing public universities and tertiary institutions. It establishes institutional governance structures (Council, Senate, Appointments Board), academic freedom frameworks, and the legal basis for staff associations and institutional autonomy.
manifestoAlignment: Supports Pillar 01 by anchoring MUBASA's collective representation within the legal framework for staff associations. Supports Pillar 04 by defining how Council and management accountability structures operate.
keyTopics: Appointments and disciplinary frameworks at law
Legal rights and duties of academic staff

title: Public Service Standing Orders 2021
summary: Government-wide regulations on public service management covering conduct, discipline, leave, retirement, and administrative procedures. MUBS staff in public service categories are governed by these orders alongside the HR Manual.
manifestoAlignment: Underpins the manifesto commitment that every member knows their rights and obligations. Pillar 01 references these orders alongside the HR Manual. Pillar 02 welfare advocacy is framed within public service rules on remuneration and conditions.
keyTopics: Rights and obligations of public servants

title: FASPU–PUNTSEF Collective Agreements
summary: Collective agreements negotiated by the Federation of Academic Staff Associations of Public Universities (FASPU) with government through PUNTSEF. They cover salary harmonisation, science pay, and national-level welfare improvements for academic staff in public universities including MUBS.
manifestoAlignment: Central to Pillar 02 (Welfare): salary harmonisation, science vs arts classification, RBS/NSSF remittance. Pillar 03 uses FASPU advocacy channels for fair promotions and national research support frameworks.
keyTopics: Collective bargaining for public university staff

title: MUBS Strategic Plan 2025–2030
summary: MUBS's five-year roadmap titled "Transforming into a Global Innovative Institution for Business and Management Education, Research and Training." It aligns the school with Uganda Vision 2040, National Development Plans, and SDGs. Key pillars include Human Capital (staff development, succession planning, digital transformation) and Sustainability.
manifestoAlignment: Pillar 03 (Growth) directly implements the Strategic Plan's Human Capital pillar — strategic staff development, succession planning, mentorship, and digital transformation including the proposed MUBASA member platform. Pillar 04 monitors management delivery of these commitments.
keyTopics: Human Capital pillar — staff development and succession planning
Monitoring and evaluation of strategic objectives
